title: Quiz: preventing duplicate payments on retry
prompt_md: A user clicks "Pay" once, but a flaky network causes their browser to retry the request, so your payment endpoint receives the same charge twice. How do you make sure the customer is only charged once?
options: Have the client send an idempotency key with the request, store it with a unique DB constraint, and return the original result on any retry instead of charging again
Disable the Pay button on the frontend after the first click
Compare the amount and customer ID; if a charge with the same amount happened in the last minute, reject it
Add a short delay before processing each payment so the duplicate arrives after the first finishes
explanation_md: The named pattern is an **idempotency key** — a unique token the client generates once per logical operation and resends on every retry. The server records it (often with a **unique constraint** on the payments table) so the first request does the charge and stores the result, while any retry with the same key returns that *same stored result* instead of charging again. This makes the operation **idempotent**: safe to repeat. Disabling a button is client-side only and bypassable; an amount/time heuristic wrongly blocks legitimate identical payments and is a race condition itself.
